title: Pilosella aletschensis
intro: Pilosella aletschensis, also known as Aletsch Saxifrage, is a rare and endangered plant species endemic to the Aletsch Glacier in Switzerland. Traditionally, Pilosella aletschensis has been used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, digestive problems, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pilosella aletschensis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat conditions such as rheumatism, bronchitis, and wound healing.
taxonomy: Pilosella aletschensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a rare and endangered plant found in the Swiss Alps. The classification of Pilosella aletschensis is part of the Pilosella genus, which is a complex group of plants that are often difficult to distinguish from one another. The exact classification of Pilosella aletschensis may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered a member of the Pilosella section of the Cichorieae tribe. Further research is needed to determine its exact classification within the Pilosella genus.
